title: Executive Order 8772—Exemption of Clarence H. Branscombe From Compulsory Retirement for Age
text:
Whereas
, in my judgment, the public interest requires that Clarence H. Branscombe, assistant supervising engineer, Public Buildings Administration, Federal Works Agency, who, during the month of August, 1941, will reach the retirement age prescribed for automatic separation from the service, applicable to him, be exempted from compulsory retirement for age for a period of one year.
Now, Therefore
, by virtue of and pursuant to the authority vested in me by section 204 of the act of June 30, 1932, 47 Stat. 382, 404 (U.S.C., title 5, sec. 715a), I hereby exempt the said Clarence H, Branscombe from compulsory retirement for age for a period of one year ending August 31, 1942.
FRANKLIN D ROOSEVELT
The White House,
June 7, 1941.
